title: Resolve merge conflicts for Persist runner preference on bead and honour it during dispatch
description:
Merge conflict detected during preflight merge of main into feature/b-7ca37c85.
Conflicted files: .takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json

Git error: Auto-merging .takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json
CONFLICT (add/add): Merge conflict in .takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

Diff context:
diff --cc .takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json
index b065d65,c702fbc..0000000
--- a/.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json
+++ b/.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json
@@@ -3,7 -3,7 +3,11 @@@
    "title": "Persist runner preference on bead and honour it during dispatch",
    "agent_type": "developer",
    "description": "When an operator runs `takt --runner claude retry <bead_id>`, the intent is to run that specific bead with the Claude runner. Currently the runner choice is not stored anywhere on the bead, so if the scheduler is later invoked with a different default (e.g. via the TUI which uses the config default), the stored preference is ignored and the bead runs with the wrong runner.\n\n## Changes required\n\n### 1. Bead model (`src/agent_takt/models.py`)\nAdd an optional field `preferred_runner: str | None = None` to the `Bead` dataclass/model. This field holds the runner name (`\"claude\"`, `\"codex\"`) explicitly chosen by the operator, or `None` if no preference was set (use config default).\n\n### 2. Retry command (`src/agent_takt/cli/commands/misc.py` or wherever `takt retry` is handled)\nWhen `takt --runner <name> retry <bead_id>` is invoked, set `bead.preferred_runner = runner_name` before saving. When `takt retry` is invoked without `--runner`, leave `preferred_runner` as `None`.\n\n### 3. Scheduler dispatch (`src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py` or `execution.py`)\nWhen dispatching a bead, if `bead.preferred_runner` is set, instantiate the runner for that backend instead of the scheduler-level default. After the bead completes (done or blocked), clear `preferred_runner` so it does not persist across retries unless explicitly set again.\n\n### 4. CLI `bead show` output\nInclude `preferred_runner` in the JSON output when it is set, so operators can see which runner a bead is pinned to.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- `takt --runner claude retry B-xyz` sets `preferred_runner = \"claude\"` on the bead and the field is visible in `takt bead show B-xyz`\n- When the scheduler dispatches a bead with `preferred_runner` set, it uses that runner regardless of the scheduler-level default\n- When the scheduler dispatches a bead with `preferred_runner = None`, it uses the scheduler-level default (existing behaviour unchanged)\n- After a bead completes or is blocked, `preferred_runner` is cleared\n- Tests cover: retry sets preferred_runner; scheduler honours preferred_runner over default; preferred_runner cleared on completion",
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +  "status": "ready",
++=======
+   "status": "done",
++>>>>>>> main
    "bead_type": "task",
    "parent_id": null,
    "dependencies": [],
@@@ -22,36 -22,98 +26,130 @@@
    "expected_files": [
      "src/agent_takt/models.py",
      "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/misc.py",
++<<<<<<< HEAD
 +    "src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py"
 +  ],
 +  "expected_globs": [],
 +  "touched_files": [],
 +  "changed_files": [],
 +  "updated_docs": [],
 +  "handoff_summary": {
 +    "completed": "",
 +    "remaining": "",
 +    "risks": "",
 +    "verdict": "",
 +    "findings_count": 0,
 +    "requires_followup": false,
 +    "changed_files": [],
++=======
+     "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/bead.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/scheduler/execution.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/scheduler/finalize.py",
+     "tests/helpers.py",
+     "tests/test_storage.py",
+     "tests/test_cli_bead.py",
+     "tests/test_recovery_flow.py",
+     "tests/test_scheduler_execution.py"
+   ],
+   "expected_globs": [],
+   "touched_files": [
+     "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/bead.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/misc.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/models.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/scheduler/execution.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/scheduler/finalize.py",
+     "tests/helpers.py",
+     "tests/test_cli_bead.py",
+     "tests/test_recovery_flow.py",
+     "tests/test_scheduler_execution.py",
+     "tests/test_storage.py"
+   ],
+   "changed_files": [
+     "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/bead.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/cli/commands/misc.py",
+     "src/agent_takt/models.py",

... (truncated)

handoff_summary.completed: Replaced `.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json` with the merged `main` version, preserving the completed status, expanded file scope, execution history, metadata, and handoff fields for bead `B-7ca37c85`.
handoff_summary.remaining: A `uv run python -m json.tool .takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json` validation attempt was blocked by sandbox access to the `uv` cache, so I did not get a tool-level parse check in this environment.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. The change is isolated to bead metadata, but any later manual overwrite of `.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json` with the stale branch copy would reintroduce the same add/add merge conflict.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: This bead resolves a metadata-only conflict. Downstream work should treat the completed `main` copy of `.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json` as authoritative and avoid restoring the stale `ready` version from the feature branch history.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose the completed bead record from `main` as the conflict resolution target instead of trying to merge individual fields manually. That preserves the authoritative status, scope, execution history, and handoff details from the bead that already finished the underlying implementation.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Could not complete a `uv run`-based JSON validation in this sandbox because `uv` was denied access to its cache directory. The file content was instead validated by inspecting the resulting diff and ensuring it exactly matches the `main` bead record.

conflict_risks: This bead resolves a metadata-only conflict. Downstream work should treat the completed `main` copy of `.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json` as authoritative and avoid restoring the stale `ready` version from the feature branch history.

execution_history.summary: Resolved the merge-conflict source by updating the local feature-root bead record to the completed metadata already present on `main`, so the branch no longer carries the stale pre-completion copy of `.takt/beads/B-7ca37c85.json`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
